collection.title: Wingspan
collection.notes: Gateway game that got me deep into the hobby. Beautiful production. Bird facts on every card.

collection.title: Terraforming Mars
collection.notes: My favorite heavy game. So many paths to victory. Every corporation feels different.

collection.title: Ticket to Ride
collection.notes: The perfect intro game. Teach it in 10 minutes, plays in an hour, everyone has fun.

collection.title: Pandemic
collection.notes: The cooperative game. Plays very differently depending on player count. Love the tension.

collection.title: Azul
collection.notes: Satisfying to play and to look at. The tile drafting is deceptively mean. Great at 2 or 4.

collection.title: Gloomhaven: Jaws of the Lion
collection.notes: Our campaign group's current obsession. The card system is brilliant. Jaws is a great entry point.

collection.title: Catan
collection.notes: Where it all started. Still plays well. Has to be 4 players, 3 is too thin.

collection.title: Root
collection.notes: Each faction plays like a completely different game. Steep learning curve but worth it. The Vagabond is chaos.

collection.title: Codenames
collection.notes: The best party game I own. Works with any group. So much replay value.

collection.title: Spirit Island
collection.notes: The best cooperative game I've played. Each spirit is wildly different. High complexity but earns it.

collection.title: Betrayal at House on the Hill
collection.notes: Some haunts are brilliant, some are broken. Chaotic and fun as a social experience regardless.

collection.title: 7 Wonders
collection.notes: Scales beautifully. 7 players, 45 minutes. The simultaneous play makes it feel faster than it is.

collection.title: Dominion
collection.notes: Invented the deck-building genre. Still one of the best implementations. Incredible variety from the expansions.

collection.title: Brass: Birmingham
collection.designer: Gavan Brown, Matt Tolman, Martin Wallace
collection.notes: Regularly tops best-of lists and it deserves it. Every decision feels meaningful. Best at 3 or 4.

collection.title: Mysterium
collection.notes: The art is stunning and the gameplay is unlike anything else. The ghost player role is harder than it looks.

play_log.notes: Jordan's bird point strategy finally clicked. Alex was robbed by the end-of-round goals.
Played Ecoline vs Mining Guild. Sam got heat flooded early and never recovered.
Finally beat Difficulty 4! Lightning + Bringer + River. Came down to the last round.
Sam played Vagabond and everyone ignored them until it was too late. Classic mistake.
Scenario 14. Brutal. Alex's Red Guard finally hit level 4.
Best clue of the night: 'orbit, 3' connecting Moon, Saturn, and Hollywood. Worked perfectly.
Jordan's canal era was untouchable. The rest of us never caught up in the rail era.
Morgan kept feeding Alex the 1-tiles on purpose. Psychological warfare.
Sam's tucking engine was unstoppable by round 3. Should have disrupted it earlier.
Three epidemics in the first two rounds. Never had a chance. Brutal start of the year.
New Year's Eve game. Morgan went full ocean tiles and it paid off. The rest of us fought over greenery.
Two-player with Marquise vs Alliance. Tight game, came down to a single point.
Casey's science strategy with Babylon went uncontested. We need to start blocking that.
Tried Difficulty 5 for the first time. Got completely overrun. Need to rethink our combo.
Village + Smithy kingdom. Drew through my deck twice in the last turn. Everything clicked.
Morgan as the ghost finally. Their visions were cryptic but we decoded them in the final round.
Scenario 12. Both characters exhausted on the last room. Need better hand management.
Got lucky with the wetland bird combo. Cached food all game and it added up.
